info.name: ID Verification Service — Tiered Pipeline (Donut)
info.description:
Postman collection for the ID Verification Service v2.0 (Port 8001).

This service uses a cost-optimised tiered pipeline:
- **Tier 1 (fast path):** PaddleOCR + regex + spatial heuristics handles ~70–90% of clean IDs.
- **Tier 2 (escalation):** Donut (naver-clova-ix/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa, ~500 MB) is only invoked for ambiguous or difficult cases.

Recommended testing order:
1. GET /health — confirm both models loaded
2. POST /debug/compare — verify both models work; see what each extracts from your test image
3. POST /verify/id (clean adult ID) — expect tier1_high, success: true
4. POST /verify/id (minor's ID) — expect age_below_minimum
5. POST /verify/id (blurry/ambiguous ID) — expect tier2_* + escalation_reason

request.description: Health check. Returns 200 when both Donut and PaddleOCR models are loaded and ready.

formdata.description: (Optional) JPEG / PNG / WEBP image of the ID document
(Optional) Direct URL to the ID document image

request.description:
Runs PaddleOCR first, then Donut — always both, regardless of what Paddle found.

Response fields:
- `dob_paddle`: what PaddleOCR + regex extracted (null if no date pattern found)
- `dob_donut`: what Donut extracted (null if it couldn't find a DoB)
- `raw_paddle_text`: full raw OCR text from PaddleOCR — useful for diagnosing regex failures

Only available when ENABLE_SWAGGER=true (dev mode).

response.name: 200 OK — Donut found DoB, Paddle did not
200 OK — Paddle found DoB, Donut did not

formdata.description: UUID — passed through from NestJS for response correlation
(Optional) JPEG / PNG / WEBP image of the ID document
(Optional) Direct URL to the ID document image

request.description:
Production tiered pipeline:
1. Tier 1 — PaddleOCR + regex (fast, no VLM)
2. Tier 2 — Donut DocVQA (escalation only)
3. Age validation — DoB must correspond to age ≥ 18

Key response fields:
- `confidence_tier`: tier1_high | tier1_low | tier2_confirmed | tier2_independent | tier2_conflict | tier2_failed
- `tier_used`: which tier resolved the request
- `escalation_reason`: why Tier 2 was triggered (null if Tier 1 resolved)

response.name: Failed — age_below_minimum
Failed — dob_extraction_failed
